# W18T1 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Create the `src/set/` module skeleton so Wave 18 has a stable module entry point and a dedicated implementation file for `unique`.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/14-set.md` §3
- `design/14-set.md` §4.1-§4.3
- `design/14-set.md` §7
- `design/01-architecture.md` §3
- `design/00-coding.md` §2.1, §2.5

## Dependencies

- W8T1, `TensorBase<S, D>` exists

## Files to Create/Modify

- `src/set/mod.rs`
- `src/set/unique.rs`
- `src/lib.rs`, add `pub mod set;` only if the crate root does not already expose the module

## Implementation Details

### Module layout

Create this file structure:

```rust
// src/set/mod.rs
mod unique;
```

```rust
// src/set/unique.rs
// placeholder for Wave 18 unique implementation
```

### Public surface for this task

- Do not add any public set operation other than the Wave 18 `unique` path.
- Keep `mod.rs` as the module entry only.
- Do not add intersection, union, difference, bincount, histogram, sorting, or counting APIs.

### Structural constraints

- Module names must stay `snake_case`.
- Keep the implementation split exactly as `mod.rs` plus `unique.rs`.
- `set` is an upper layer consumer of `tensor`, `storage`, `dimension`, `element`, `complex`, and `iter`. Do not introduce reverse dependencies.

## Test Plan

- `src/set/mod.rs` compile smoke test through crate build
- No behavior test is required yet beyond successful module compilation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/set/mod.rs` exists
- [ ] `src/set/unique.rs` exists
- [ ] The crate exposes the `set` module from `src/lib.rs` if needed
- [ ] No extra public set APIs are introduced
- [ ] The crate compiles with the new module skeleton

## Development Workflow

1. Add `src/set/mod.rs` with the `mod unique;` declaration.
2. Add `src/set/unique.rs` as an empty or placeholder implementation file.
3. Wire the module into `src/lib.rs` if missing.
4. Run the smallest compile check.
5. Leave all actual `unique` trait and method work for W18T2 and W18T3.
